agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive 2025 Homeless Count report for Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive a report and presentation on the 2025 Homeless Count for Vancouver. This is an information item only — no vote or bylaw is proposed at this meeting. The report will provide updated data on homelessness in the region, which may inform future policy or funding decisions that could affect local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	2025 Homeless Count – Vancouver
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
Presentation
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 Cultural Grants for Operating & Spaces
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed 2026 Cultural Grants that would provide funding for arts and culture organizations in Burnaby. The grants cover two areas: operating costs and cultural spaces. Because some of the recommendations require a special two-thirds majority vote under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter (a rule that demands extra support from council members for certain decisions), this is not a done deal yet. The decision is still upcoming, and the outcome depends on the council vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on these grants — if approved, they could affect funding for your arts or cultural organization.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	2026 Cultural Grants (Balance of Operating, Cultural Spaces)
Recommendations A to C, E and G require at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants for Spring 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to award grants to community sport events happening in Vancouver during the Spring of 2026. This is part of the Vancouver Community Sport Event Grant program. The recommendation requires at least two-thirds of all council members to vote 'yes' to pass, as per Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter (a special rule for certain council decisions). If approved, the grants would help fund local sporting events that benefit the community.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you run a community sport event in Vancouver, watch for the council decision and prepare to apply for a Spring 2026 grant if the program is approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants – Spring 2026 Intake
Recommendation A requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby property tax distribution for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on how to divide the total property tax levy among different classes of properties (like homes, businesses, and industry) for the 2026 tax year. This decision will determine how much of the overall tax burden falls on commercial and residential properties. A proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for each property class. This is not yet approved — the vote will happen at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it will affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	2026 Property Taxation: Distribution of Property Tax Levy
4a. Budget Resolution
4b. Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 property tax exemptions for non-profits
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a report that recommends granting property tax exemptions for 2025 to certain non-profit organizations in Burnaby. A property tax exemption means the organization does not have to pay property tax on the land or building it uses. This decision would affect which groups get the break and how much tax revenue the city gives up. The report is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if you run a non-profit, this could affect your operating costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.	2025 Property Tax Exemptions
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Coastal Flood Management Policy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed Coastal Flood Management Policy. This policy would set new rules for building and development in areas of Burnaby that are at risk of flooding from the ocean. If approved, the policy could affect property owners, developers, and businesses near the coast by requiring flood-proofing measures and limiting what can be built. The goal is to protect people and property from rising sea levels and storm surges. Council will consider the report at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if your property is in a coastal flood risk area.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.	Coastal Flood Management Policy
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider Vancouver Heritage Foundation annual report and board reappointment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a recommendation to reappoint members to the Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board. This recommendation requires at least two-thirds of all council members to vote in favour, as stated under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter. The decision is not yet final — it will be decided at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.	Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board – Annual Report 2025
Recommendation B requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of theVancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to choose plazas for 2026–2029 public drinking program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on which public plazas will be part of the Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program for 2026 through 2029. This proposed program would allow people to drink alcohol in certain outdoor public spaces during specific hours. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider a staff report and may approve a list of sites. If approved, the program would run for three years starting in 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if your business is near a proposed plaza, this could affect foot traffic or noise levels.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.	Sites for 2026-2029 Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby seeks grant to speed up development approvals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider applying for a provincial grant under the Local Government Development Approvals Program. If approved, the funding would help the city hire more staff and upgrade its systems to process building and development permits faster. This is not a done deal yet — council must vote on whether to submit the application.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for council's decision on whether to apply for the grant.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.	Funding Application to Local Government Development Approvals Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on housing rezoning rule changes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a package of miscellaneous amendments that would change the conditions attached to various housing rezoning approvals in Burnaby. A proposed bylaw would update rules for things like affordable housing requirements, density bonuses, and community amenity contributions. These changes are not yet in effect — council will consider and vote on them at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the new conditions would apply to future rezoning applications, potentially affecting how much developers must contribute to community benefits and what types of housing get built.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you are a developer or property owner with a pending rezoning application.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
10.	Miscellaneous Amendments Concerning Various Housing Rezoning Conditions
(no speakers per section 7.2(b) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Text amendment to CD-1 (839) bylaw for 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed text amendment to the Comprehensive Development (CD-1) Bylaw No. 13493 for properties at 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue. A CD-1 bylaw is a special zoning rule that allows a specific development. This amendment would change the rules for that site. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect development rules for these properties.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.	CD-1 (839) Text Amendment: 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue
(no speakers per section 7.2(b) of the Procedure By-law)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning at 4088 Granville Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 4088 Granville Street. The rezoning would change the land-use rules for this specific site, allowing a different type of development than what is currently permitted. This is a 'CD-1' (Comprehensive Development) rezoning, which means the city would create a custom set of zoning rules just for this property. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the draft bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 3803-3823 West 10th Ave and 2553 Highbury St
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for properties at 3803-3823 West 10th Avenue and 2553 Highbury Street. The plan would change the zoning rules for these lots through a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) bylaw. A bylaw is a local law passed by the city. This rezoning would also require updates to the city's main Zoning and Development Bylaw and the Sign Bylaw. The proposal is still under review and has not been approved yet.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 310 East 14th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 310 East 14th Avenue. This is a 'CD-1' rezoning, which stands for Comprehensive Development Zone — a special zoning category that allows for a specific development plan rather than following standard zoning rules. The draft bylaw would amend Burnaby's Zoning and Development Bylaw. The decision has not been made yet; this item is on the council meeting agenda for discussion and a vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and development plans.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning at 1305-1325 West 13th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1305-1325 West 13th Avenue. A 'rezoning' means changing the official rules about what can be built or done on that land. This is a first step — the draft bylaw is up for approval at a council meeting. If passed, it would amend the city's main Zoning and Development Bylaw. No public speakers are scheduled for this item under the council's procedure rules.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property values or development plans.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby amenity cost charge bylaw and density bonus updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider updates to how the city collects money from new developments to pay for community amenities like parks, community centres, and childcare facilities. A proposed Amenity Cost Charge bylaw would set standard fees for developers, replacing the current system of negotiating each project individually. Council will also consider updates to the Density Bonus program, which allows developers to build more units in exchange for providing community benefits. These changes are part of the city's 2026 Financing Growth Update and are not yet approved. A public consultation process is required before any final decision is made.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how new development fees will be set in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
16.	2026 Financing Growth Update – Required Consultation for Amenity Cost Charge By law and Bill 16 Density Bonus and Inclusionary Zoning Updates
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw to assess property for Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square maintenance costs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge property owners in the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square area for the 2025 maintenance costs of those public spaces. This is part of a 'Local Improvement Project' — a way for the city to pay for upgrades or upkeep by billing the properties that benefit directly. The bylaw has not been approved yet; it is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Check if your property is in the Trounce Alley or Blood Alley Square area — if so, watch for the council vote on this bylaw.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 maintenance costs for the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square Local Improvement Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw to assess property costs for East Hastings parking project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge certain property owners in the East Hastings Street area for the costs of a shared parking project in 2025. The bylaw would 'assess real property' — meaning it would add a special fee or tax to specific properties to pay for the collective parking project. This is not yet approved; council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, property owners in the affected area may face a new fee for the parking project.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 costs for the East Hastings Street Collective Parking Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a Housing Agreement for the property at 320-360 West 2nd Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in how the building will be used — in this case, to ensure the units remain as affordable rental housing for a set number of years. This is not yet approved; council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 6.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Amend housing agreement for 450-496 Prior Street, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change an existing Housing Agreement for properties at 450-496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue, and 1002 Station Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a developer that sets rules for keeping rental units affordable. This amendment would update the terms of that agreement. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you are a tenant or owner affected by this housing agreement.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 5.	A By-law to authorize the amendment of a Housing Agreement Authorized by By-law No. 14507 (450 – 496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue and 1002 Station Street)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Amend CD-1 (198) bylaw for 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the rules for a specific property at 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue. This property is currently zoned under a special 'Comprehensive Development' bylaw (CD-1 (198) Bylaw No. 6254). The proposed amendment would alter what is allowed on that land. The exact changes are not detailed in the agenda item, but any change to a CD-1 bylaw can affect what a business owner can build, operate, or do on that site. Council will consider this amendment at an upcoming meeting. Note: Councillor Montague must review the proceedings before being eligible to vote on this matter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or operate a business at or near 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue, contact the Burnaby Planning Department to learn how this proposed change could affect your property.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	A By-law to amend CD-1 (198) By-law No. 6254 (1700-1750 West 75th Avenue)
(Councillor Montague will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 1710-1730 East Pender Street to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone 1710-1730 East Pender Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows the city to set specific rules for a particular property, rather than following standard zoning rules. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet. Council will consider the bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this rezoning could affect nearby property values or future development plans in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (1710-1730 East Pender Street)
(Councillor Maloney, Orr and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 6486 Chester Street to CD-1 in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 6486 Chester Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for specific development plans not covered by standard zoning rules. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet. Council will consider the bylaw at an upcoming meeting. Councillors Bligh, Fry, and Mayor Sim must review the proceedings before they are eligible to vote on this matter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own or operate a business near 6486 Chester Street.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (6486 Chester Street)
(Councillors Bligh, Fry and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 1302-1318 East 12th Avenue to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1302-1318 East 12th Avenue to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for a specific development plan, rather than following the standard rules for that area. This is a first step — the bylaw has not been approved yet, and Council will debate and decide on it at a future meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if you own property nearby or have an interest in this development, attend the hearing to voice your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (1302 - 1318 East 12th Avenue)
(Councillors Klassen, Maloney, Montague, Orr and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a proposed rezoning for properties at 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street. A 'CD-1 Rezoning' would change the official land-use designation for these sites, allowing a specific development project to move forward. This is a proposal only — it has not been approved yet. The Council will review a referral report and decide whether to move the application to a public hearing.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if you own property nearby or run a business in the area, you may want to attend or submit comments.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	CD-1 Rezoning: 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street
Referral Report

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 rezoning for 888 West 8th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for the property at 888 West 8th Avenue. A referral report has been prepared, meaning the city is considering changing the zoning to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. This would allow a specific development plan for that site. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on whether to move forward with this rezoning proposal.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to have a say on the proposed rezoning.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	CD-1 Rezoning: 888 West 8th Avenue

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Fast-track rental housing and small hotels in new Burnaby mixed-use district
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to create a new 'Mixed-Use Commercial District' that would fast-track approvals for rental housing and small hotels. If approved, the new rules would allow developers to build rental apartments and small hotels (under a certain size) more quickly, without needing a full rezoning process for each project. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet. Council will consider the report at an upcoming meeting, and there may be a public hearing before a final vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to tell Council if you support or oppose the new district rules.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	Fast-Tracking Rental Housing and Small Hotels – New Mixed-Use Commercial District
Referral Report
